text.title: risk-register Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for risk-register as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: One-time exercise — risks identified at kickoff, register never updated.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Project context evolves weekly; a static register reflects the world as it was, not as it is. Stale scores give false comfort and miss emerging threats entirely.

failure-mode.description: No owners — risks listed without a named accountable person.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unowned risks are nobody's problem. When they activate, the team scrambles to find who should respond, costing days in crisis mode before a response is even designed.

failure-mode.description: All-Accept strategy — every high-score risk marked "Accept" with no contingency funded.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Accept without contingency is wishful thinking. High-score risks that materialize hit the project budget as emergency overruns with no pre-approved response plan.

failure-mode.description: Missing positive risks — register covers only threats.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Opportunities that pass unnoticed are value left on the table. A threat-only register uses 50% of the risk management value.

failure-mode.description: Too vague — "Something might go wrong with the vendor" is not an actionable risk.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vague descriptions prevent probability/impact analysis and make response planning impossible. Every risk needs a specific "what might happen and why" framing.
